rag_rules: Insert AGREE annex blocks into the target implementation scope when binding implementation-level details, and into the component type scope for port-level assumptions and guarantees.
generic_rules: Preserve AADL structure by only referencing ports, subcomponents, and connections that are explicitly declared in the input model.
Place implementation-level bindings after subcomponents and connections in the implementation scope.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use `annex agree {** ... **};` syntax for all AGREE blocks.
Ensure range comparisons use numeric scalars and avoid Boolean or opaque port types unless explicitly typed as bool in AGREE scope.
anti_patterns: Do not reference AADL properties (e.g., Period, Deadline) as if they are AGREE variables.
Avoid using subcomponent paths in component type annexes unless mapped through visible ports or outputs.
irrelevant_topics: type scope range assumption and guarantee
AADL Property Names Are Not AGREE Variables
